data.title: Sports and lottery websites headed to Toronto for 2025 street battle finale, drivers Callum Ilott and Louis Foster.
data.description: A recent report reveals that the global internet penetration rate has reached a new milestone of 60 percent, with over 4.7 billion people now having internet access. Additionally, the number of social media users has exceeded 4.3 billion, indicating a significant increase in online communication and connectivity. The report also highlights that mobile phone usage continues to rise, with more than 5.1 billion unique mobile users worldwide. These numbers demonstrate a growing reliance on digital technology for communication and access to information on a global scale.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller supports July rate cut, ready to replace Powell if Trump chooses. He backs job shift by 2026, citing need based on labor market data.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller strongly advocated for a rate cut in July and expressed readiness to potentially take over as Federal Reserve Chair if President Trump offers him the position. In a recent interview on Bloomberg TV, Waller, along with fellow policymaker Michelle Bowman, voiced support for a rate cut in the upcoming July meeting, aligning with the President's call for looser monetary policy. Waller not only endorsed a rate reduction but also stated his willingness to assume the role of Federal Reserve Chair in 2026 if nominated. This comes amidst mounting speculation about Jerome Powell's future. Waller emphasized the urgency for a rate cut due to concerning labor market indicators, despite seemingly stable headline figures. His stance highlights a shift towards a more accommodative monetary policy amid economic uncertainties. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Snap-on Inc. (NYSE: SNA) beats Q2 earnings; $1.179 billion in net sales (YoY increase) and $4.72 EPS; Analysts revise estimates.
data.description: Snap-On Inc. (NYSE: SNA) reported second-quarter 2025 results on Thursday, exceeding revenue and earnings consensus. The quarterly net sales stood at $1.179 billion, a flat year-over-year increase, surpassing the estimated $1.16 billion. The EPS for the quarter was $4.72, slightly down from $5.07 YoY but higher than the expected $4.67. Snap-On chairman and CEO, Nick Pinchuk, expressed optimism over the results, attributing the success to returning sales growth in the U.S. Tools Group, resilient gross margins, and solid operating earnings performance amidst ongoing challenges. Despite general uncertainty and trade turbulence, the company maintained its positive trajectory. Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Perficient names Rajesh Mehta as CFO, replacing retiring Paul Martin; effective immediately.
data.description: In Saint Louis and New York, Perficient has announced the appointment of Rajesh Mehta as the new Chief Financial Officer. Mehta will be stepping into this role immediately, taking over from Paul Martin, who is retiring.

data.title: Traditional finance and fintech firms are racing for market leadership in tokenizing real-world assets like U.S. Treasury bonds, real estate, and private equity. Wall Street firms such as BlackRock and Goldman Sachs are diving in, promising more liquidity and security. Pharos Network, a blockchain platform for regulated assets, launched its testnet on March 6, 2025. Tokenization aims to make assets tradable, fractional, and programmable, changing how value is transferred to investors. This trend offers a new wave of investment opportunities for retail investors within the rules of traditional finance. (Word count: 107)
data.description: Traditional finance and new finance are competing for market dominance in the tokenization space. This involves converting real-world assets into digital tokens on a blockchain, creating vast investment opportunities. Fintech companies like Polymath have been leading this charge, with innovations like Polymesh for regulated assets. Pharos Network, founded by former AntChain and Alibaba blockchain leaders, aims to revolutionize value transfer in the investment world. Key players like BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, and Goldman Sachs are offering digital versions of traditional assets, indicating a shift towards tokenization. The article emphasizes the benefits of tokenization, such as increased liquidity, accessibility, and security, as Wall Street moves towards this trend.
AMEX CEO States Stablecoins Are More Like Investment Options and Will Not Replace Traditional Money .

data.title: Netflix reports Q2 revenue of $11.08B (up 16% YoY), beats estimates. Raises full-year revenue guidance to $44.8-45.2B.
data.description: Netflix Inc. (NASDAQ: NFLX) released their second-quarter financial results, showing revenue of $11.08 billion, a 16% increase from the previous year, surpassing the expected $11.04 billion. Earnings per share for the quarter stood at $7.19, higher than the projected $7.06. The company anticipates third-quarter revenue to reach $11.526 billion, a 17% increase year-over-year, with an estimated earnings per share of $6.87. These forecasts exceed Street estimates. Additionally, Netflix raised its full-year revenue guidance to a range of $44.8 billion to $45.2 billion from the earlier projection of $43.5 billion. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cintas beat Q4 earnings with $1.09 EPS, sales of $2.667B (+8% YoY). Analysts increase their forecasts post upbeat results.
data.description: Cintas Corporation (NASDAQ: CTAS) exceeded expectations in its fourth-quarter financial report by posting earnings per share of $1.09, surpassing the analyst consensus of $1.07. The quarterly sales figures were also impressive, reaching $2.667 billion which reflects an 8% increase compared to the previous year and outperforming the Street's prediction of $2.626 billion. CEO Todd M. Schneider expressed the company's commitment to providing excellent service to customers, leveraging their unique culture, and delivering sustainable outcomes for stakeholders in the upcoming fiscal year 2026. Cintas anticipates the GAAP EPS for fiscal 2026 to fall within the range of $4.71.

data.title: Post-it maker 3M defies tariff impact, reports Q2 sales of $6.3B, a 1.4% increase YoY.Stock surges after raising full-year guidance.
data.description: 3M Company (NYSE: MMM) saw a rise in its stock on Friday following the release of its exceeding second-quarter 2025 results and an increase in its full-year guidance. The company recorded GAAP sales worth $6.3 billion, indicating a 1.4% increase compared to the previous year. Adjusted sales were reported at $6.2 billion, showcasing a 1.5% organic growth that surpassed the estimated $6.09 billion. This quarter marked the company's third consecutive growth period across all three business groups, with 13 out of 16 divisions experiencing positive growth. The impressive performance was mainly driven by the electronics and industrials sectors, with China showing a mid-single-digit increase and EMEA staying steady. Moreover, the adjusted operating margin rose by 290 basis points to 24.5%, supported by growth, productivity enhancements, and reduced restructuring expenses. These improvements were slightly hindered by investments, tariffs, and foreign exchange impacts.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories beat Q2 sales & profit expectations, reporting $11.14 billion in sales & adjusted earnings of $1.26. CEO projects momentum into 2026. Earnings guidance for 2025 adjusted.
data.description: Abbott Laboratories publicly traded as ABT recently released its second-quarter financial results, surpassing market expectations. The company reported sales of $11.14 billion for the quarter, exceeding the consensus of $11.07 billion. Their adjusted earnings stood at $1.26 per share, higher than the anticipated $1.25, aligning with their forecast range of $1.23 to $1.27. Chairman and CEO Robert B. Ford expressed satisfaction with achieving high single-digit organic sales growth, double-digit EPS growth, improved margins, and progress in product development halfway through the year. Despite the positive performance, Abbott adjusted its full-year 2025 earnings projections. The company anticipates maintaining its current momentum through to 2026. For further details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Global market for Data Center Fire Detection and Suppression estimated at $1.3B in 2024, projected to reach $1.8B by 2030 - 5.8% CAGR growth.
data.description: A report on Data Center Fire Detection and Suppression has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global market size for this sector was valued at approximately US$1.3 Billion in 2024 and is anticipated to grow to US$1.8 Billion by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.8% from 2024 to 2030. This detailed report offers insights into market trends, drivers, and projections, aiding in making well-informed business decisions.

data.title: Greenidge Generation completed Senior Notes due 2026 Tender/Exchange Offer with final results, 8.50% Old Notes involved.
data.description: In Pittsford, New York, Greenidge Generation Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: GREE) disclosed the outcomes of its recent Tender/Exchange Offer. The company, known for its cryptocurrency datacenter and power generation operations, completed the offers to exchange or buy back its 8.50% Senior Notes due 2026 (referred to as the Old Notes). The Offer allowed holders to choose between exchanging or selling their Old Notes. The final results of this Offer were officially reported by Greenidge Generation Holdings Inc.
Suzan DelBene reports recent stock transactions.

data.title: XLM shows most bullish chart in crypto, with high correlation coefficient above 0.70 with XRP during bull runs. Will history repeat?
data.description: In past bull markets, XLM and XRP frequently demonstrate a strong correlation, often with a correlation coefficient exceeding 0.70. The question now is whether Stellar will follow suit this time around.
